calendar.matters:
Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust, Removal Of Trustee, Appointing Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement [Ccp 664.6]
Off Calendar Dec-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hedemark for trustee Norman Stewart, Jr., who was also present.
Tanis Kidd, beneficiary in pro per.
Sonja Stewart, interested party.
Alfred Stewart, interested party.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) First Amended Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement; and 2) Petition for Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust, Removal of Trustee, Appointing Temporary Trustee.

The Court stated that the petition appeared well-framed and asked whether any person objected to approval of the settlement. No objections were made.

The Court confirmed with Mr. Hedemark that approval of the settlement would resolve and remove from calendar the petition for judicial proceedings and removal of the trustee. Both counsel and Ms. Kidd indicated agreement that the petition should be taken off calendar as a result of the settlement. The Court also confirmed that the petition for letters of administration filed by Ms. Kidd was no longer necessary due to the global resolution achieved through the settlement. No party disagreed. All parties agreed that approval of the settlement was in everyone's best interests.

There being no objection, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Off Calendar

Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust, Removal Of Trustee, Appointing Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust, Removal Of Trustee, Appointing Temporary
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Continued To Sep-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tanis Kidd, Petitioner in Pro Per.
Justin Hedemark for and with Norman Stewart, Jr., Respondent/Trustee.
Sonja Stewart, Heir.
Treasure Clark, Heir.
Albert Stewart and Anassa Stewart were also present.

The Court heard argument on the petition for judicial proceedings concerning trust administration. Petitioner and other interested parties were present and discussed their positions on the distribution of trust property, the validity and effect of a 2019 family agreement, and the contents of the Living Trust.

Mr. Hedemark and Ms. Kidd confirmed they had reviewed the tentative ruling. Ms. Kidd reported she had prepared a proposed order and supplement and would process them with the clerk's office and mail notice and supplemental information to all interested parties. She requested a continuance to allow time to provide notice and serve required Zoom appearance information per local rules.

The Court inquired about prior settlement discussions and heard statements from various family members regarding their interests, disagreements over the proposed distribution, past agreements, and the valuation of trust assets. Family members discussed past arrangements regarding two residential properties, their respective contributions, and intentions to buy out one another's interest.

Following discussion, the Court found the matter suitable for referral to pro bono mediation and ordered participation of all parties, including those with interests in related estate matters, including in the related Estate of Norman Stewart Sr. (PES-20-303558)and Estate of Barbara Stewart (PES-20-303559) cases. Mediation shall be completed by September 16, 2025. A report on the status of mediation shall be provided to the assigned probate examiner by September 19, 2025. The Court set a hearing on status of mediation for September 23, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The parties were informed that a copy of the Minute Order Referring Case to Pro Bono Mediation could be obtained from the court's website after the hearing.

The Court stated that it will issue a Breslin Order requiring notice of the mediation to all interested parties. The underlying petition is also continued to September 23, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust
Off Calendar Mar-28-2024 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-28-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Justin Hedemark appeared via zoom for and with Norman Stewart Jr. in person (petitioner).
Tanis Kidd appeared in person (pro per).

Discussion was held regarding the living trust, that Ms. Kidd has not seen the living trust, and that she objects to having Norman Stewart Jr. as trustee. Petition for order and Petition for judicial proceedings concerning trust are taken under submission.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Jaime. Not reported.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Continued To Mar-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust
Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Continued To Mar-26-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Judicial Proceedings Concerning Trust
Off Calendar Feb-21-2024 Continued To Mar-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For An Order To Transfer Trust Assets And Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Nov-15-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Justin Hedemark, via ZOOM, for Norman Stewart Jr., in court, Trustee; Kevin Buggy, via ZOOM, for Tanis Kidd, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

The court queries counsel regarding why the Egbert St. property was not transferred to the trust. Mr. Buggy reports that the family is familiar regarding the decedent's intent to transfer the Egbert property to the trust and points the court to Norman Stewart Jr.'s declaration regarding the preceding, filed with the court. Mr. Hedemark reports that the family agrees regarding the intent of the decedent regarding distribution but regrettably, the family has been unable to locate the trust document. The court advises the parties that the question is whether the decedent intended to transfer the Egbert property to the trust. Norman Stewart Jr. provides oral testimony regarding his father's plan to do so but that his father passed away before he could do so.

After additional discussion, the Petition for an Order to Transfer Trust Assets, etc. is submitted. Mr. Buggy and the court briefly discuss the proposed order previously submitted and advises that he may need to submit a new proposed order to the court.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
